trends_result.evidence: Interest: 18/100 now vs 0/100 six weeks ago · Peak: 100/100 · geo: IN · 90 days

social_result.evidence: Web-indexed social mentions · 5 results · Note: search results about social content, not direct Instagram data

news_result.evidence: 0 articles in past 2 months · 0 fashion-relevant signals

india_fit.price_band_reason: Angrakha kurtis are traditionally constructed and can be mass-produced at ₹399–₹1,499 without quality compromise.
india_fit.climate_fit_reason: Angrakha's layered, overlapping front panel adds bulk in Indian heat; better suited to cooler months or festive wear than everyday summer.
india_fit.cultural_fit_reason: Angrakha is a historically rooted, modest silhouette deeply aligned with Indian aesthetic norms and regional preferences.
india_fit.value_fashion_fit_reason: Meesho/Vishal customers recognize ethnic kurtis, but angrakha's construction complexity and lower current marketplace traction (10 listings, no launches) suggest unproven bulk demand at this price tier.

claude_synthesis.convergence_summary: Google Trends shows real recent search interest (spike to 100/100), but marketplace listings remain thin, news coverage is absent, and social buzz is minimal—suggesting emerging awareness that has not yet consolidated into retail inventory or sales velocity.
claude_synthesis.signal_agreement: Partial agreement: Demand (Google Trends) shows momentum; buzz (news, social) shows minimal editorial endorsement; marketplace supply (Myntra/Meesho) is cautious and unchanged.
claude_synthesis.disagreement_note: Direct sales data (sell-through rate, repeat purchase rate, returns) from Myntra/Meesho for angrakha kurtis in the ₹399–₹1,499 band would clarify whether the Google Trends spike reflects genuine purchase intent or passing curiosity. SKU-level velocity would resolve this.
claude_synthesis.bet_reasoning: The 18→100 Google Trends jump in 90 days is a genuine early-stage demand signal that warrants cautious inventory; however, the complete absence of news coverage, thin marketplace catalog (10 SKUs), and lack of brand launches suggest this is still pre-mass-market. Recommend a small, fast-turning test batch (200–400 units at ₹599–₹999) to capture upside without overcommitting to unvalidated demand.
claude_synthesis.skepticism_flag: Google Trends spikes can reflect one-time seasonal searches (e.g., wedding season, festival prep) or search-engine gaming rather than sustained category demand—the zero-launch signal from major marketplaces is a concrete red flag that even aggregators are not seeing profitable velocity yet.
